title: pterocarpus hanceanus
intro: Pterocarpus hanceanus, also known as the Nanmu or Chinese red sandalwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a tree native to southern China and Taiwan, valued for its distinctive heartwood and medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Pterocarpus hanceanus can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall health and well-being.
